label: U.S. Foreign Policy & Diplomacy
vocabulary: FRUS (Foreign Relations of the United States)

sources.title: The U.S. and the Modern World

app_prompts: US foreign policy doctrine quiz: user reads a historical scenario and identifies which doctrine applied — Monroe, Big Stick, Dollar Diplomacy, Open Door, containment — and sees what actually happened.
FRUS document explorer: user picks a diplomatic crisis and steps through the archival record — what State Department cables said, what the President knew, what was hidden from Congress.
American imperialism timeline: user traces US overseas expansion from 1898 to the present — Philippines, Panama, Cuba, Vietnam, Iraq — and sees the justifications used each time.
Transnational history mapper: user picks a 20th-century movement (anti-colonialism, non-alignment, human rights) and traces how it crossed borders and shaped US foreign policy responses.

Foreign policy decision-making case study: user picks a crisis (Cuban Missile Crisis, Gulf of Tonkin, Iraq 2003) and walks through the decision process — who advised what, what was known, what was assumed.
